summary: Re-linked the Dolphin AI runtime library by changing `dolphin/ai/ai.c` from `NonMatching` to `Matching` in `configure.py`. The source adjustment replaced `#ifdef MUST_MATCH` guards with `#ifdef MWERKS_GEKKO` around the MWERKS-specific stack variable and inline assembly callback stack switch, allowing the object to be treated as matching while keeping compiler-specific code gated correctly.
what_changed: `configure.py` changed the DolphinLib entry for `ai` from `Object(NonMatching, "dolphin/ai/ai.c")` to `Object(Matching, "dolphin/ai/ai.c")`.
`src/dolphin/ai/ai.c` changed the guard for `static u8* __OldStack;` from `#ifdef MUST_MATCH` to `#ifdef MWERKS_GEKKO`.
`src/dolphin/ai/ai.c` changed the guard for the inline assembly function `__AICallbackStackSwitch(register AIDCallback cb)` from `#ifdef MUST_MATCH` to `#ifdef MWERKS_GEKKO`.
smart_moves: Separated matching status from compiler-specific source inclusion: the inline assembly and stack-switch support are gated on `MWERKS_GEKKO`, not the broader project matching flag `MUST_MATCH`.
Kept the relink narrowly scoped: only the object classification and two preprocessor guards changed.
Preserved the MWERKS inline assembly path needed for matching while avoiding tying that path to the object's `Matching`/`NonMatching` bookkeeping state.
decomp_lessons: When promoting an object from `NonMatching` to `Matching`, audit any `MUST_MATCH` guards; they may be better expressed as compiler guards such as `MWERKS_GEKKO` if the guarded code is compiler-specific rather than match-status-specific.
Inline assembly needed for MWERKS/CodeWarrior builds should generally be controlled by compiler availability, not by whether the file is currently classified as matching.
A relink PR can be very small: if the source already matches, the main work may be updating the object status and fixing conditional compilation so the matching build path remains correct.
naming_conventions: Dolphin AI internal callbacks use double-underscore-prefixed names such as `__AIS_Callback`, `__AID_Callback`, `__CallbackStack`, and `__OldStack`.
The interrupt handler and stack switch helper follow Dolphin-style internal naming: `__AIDHandler` and `__AICallbackStackSwitch`.
Build configuration uses `DolphinLib("ai", [...])` with `Object(Matching, "dolphin/ai/ai.c")` for matched runtime library objects.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Use `Object(Matching, "dolphin/ai/ai.c")` in `configure.py` once the object can be linked as matching.
Gate MWERKS-only inline assembly with `#ifdef MWERKS_GEKKO`.
Do not rely on `#ifdef MUST_MATCH` for code that is fundamentally compiler-specific, such as `static asm` routines.
The affected assembly helper is `static asm void __AICallbackStackSwitch(register AIDCallback cb)`, which begins with `fralloc` in the shown diff excerpt.
follow_up_queries: Find other files that use `#ifdef MUST_MATCH` around MWERKS-only inline assembly and consider whether `MWERKS_GEKKO` is the more accurate guard.
Check remaining `Object(NonMatching, "dolphin/...`) runtime library entries for small guard or build-configuration changes that could allow relinking.
Audit Dolphin runtime libraries for objects whose matching status is blocked only by conditional compilation rather than source equivalence.
